title: PR #9 Codex-review round-3 follow-ups (exit-preempt CREATED-buy self-cross; protection fail-closed; late-fill cleanup parity)
reason: Three pre-existing R2 safety-surface gaps Codex surfaced on the PR #9 delta, each empirically confirmed. F3 (P1 self-cross): the exit-preempt stand-down only expired PENDING/APPROVED buy candidates, so a same-symbol BUY already dispatched to a CREATED order under an ORDERED candidate was neither stood down nor blocking (MAY_EXECUTE excludes CREATED) and could claim to re-grow the position after the exit SELL went terminal; flatten already prevents this (FLATTEN_BLOCKING includes CREATED). Fix: a companion _stand_down_symbol_created_buys_* locally cancels same-symbol unexecuted (filled_quantity==0) CREATED buys in the exit's atomic unit, both stores, leaving the documented MAY_EXECUTE design intact. F1 (P1): open_protection_exit minted the PROTECTION_FLOOR SELL even when a same-symbol buy may execute, so the claim rail wedged it or it was mis-sized if the buy filled; fix fails closed (returns None + audited protection_open_deferred) on a MAY_EXECUTE buy so the loop retries, both stores. F2 (P2 parity): a late fill on an already-terminal envelope skipped memory's staged-child cancel + owner-reconcile (nested under the transition-only branch) that SQLite runs unconditionally on terminal; fix keys memory's cleanup on stored.status terminal, matching SQLite. Red-first + mutation-verified (5 mutations, in-place edit-back); full native gate + both oracles + hardening gates + perf gate (limits unchanged) + AI-OS hygiene green. Pushed to consolidate/r2-canonical (PR #9 head); Codex PR reviewer re-reviews; operator ratifies the F1/F3 design choices and merges after clean re-review.
